channel.title: Visual Story on anastasiiamorozova
channel.description: Recent content in Visual Story on anastasiiamorozova

item.title: How I created an ML model to uncover drug-trafficking on Telegram
item.description: I scraped 500,000 messages from 1,500 Telegram channels using Telethon and a snowballing method, then built a Doc2Vec classifier trained on 300 manually labeled drug ads to identify advertisements at scale. The investigation showed that Poland’s online drug market runs on Telegram, InPost parcel lockers, and BLIK — the full story is here, and below is how I built it.

item.title: Mapping Russia's War Machine on NATO's Doorstep
item.description: We used high-resolution satellite imagery to map Russian military base activity along NATO’s eastern border. GRU special forces in Kaliningrad are being trained for sabotage operations, and new nuclear weapons infrastructure is taking shape in Belarus.

item.title: Online neo-Nazi Store and Customers From Central Europe Exposed
item.description: A database of 20,000 purchases from the neo-Nazi webshop Midgård was leaked by the antifascist organization AFA. We analyzed the customer register to map how widespread its Central European sales network is, identifying buyers across Poland, Czechia, and Slovakia.

item.title: Unpacking CEE's biggest oil company's promises to go green
item.description: Orlen presents itself as a green energy leader. We dug into company filings and public data and found a company fighting to preserve oil refining profits, using Poland’s slow green transition as cover. Published as a scrollytelling piece.
